summary: Critical security vulnerabilities and TypeScript errors found across the Glan Getaway booking platform. Top 3 issues: 1) 31 high-severity dependency vulnerabilities across frontend and backend, 2) Hardcoded Stripe test key in frontend .env file, 3) 69 TypeScript compilation errors preventing type safety.

issues.title: Hardcoded Stripe test key in frontend .env file
issues.description: The frontend .env file contains a hardcoded Stripe test publishable key (pk_test_qhbySbatHadEroU67YNHanX9). While this is a test key, hardcoded credentials in version control is a security anti-pattern and could accidentally be committed with production keys.
issues.root_cause: Stripe key committed to .env file which should be excluded from version control or use environment-specific values
issues.fix_suggestion: Remove the hardcoded key from .env and ensure .env is properly gitignored. Use environment variables in deployment platforms (Vercel, Render, etc.)

issues.title: 31 high-severity dependency vulnerabilities
issues.description: Backend has 16 vulnerabilities (4 low, 3 moderate, 9 high) and frontend has 15 vulnerabilities (6 moderate, 9 high). Critical vulnerabilities include: React Router XSS via Open Redirects, Axios DoS attacks, Rollup arbitrary file write, Vite multiple path traversal vulnerabilities, Cloudinary argument injection, express-rate-limit IPv6 bypass, lodash prototype pollution, and validator.js URL bypass.
issues.root_cause: Outdated dependencies with known security vulnerabilities
evidence: Critical packages: axios, react-router-dom, vite, rollup, cloudinary, express-rate-limit, lodash, validator
issues.fix_suggestion: Run npm audit fix for automatic fixes. For breaking changes, manually update vulnerable packages to latest secure versions. Test thoroughly after updates.

patch.diff: Run: npm audit fix && npm audit fix --force (if needed)

issues.title: 69 TypeScript compilation errors
issues.description: The codebase has 69 TypeScript errors preventing compilation and type safety. Issues include: unused variables/imports, implicit any types, missing type definitions, and type mismatches. Key errors include: missing BookingType properties (isPwdBooking, isSeniorCitizenBooking), implicit any types in FreshAccommodationDisplay and ManageHotelForm, and type incompatibilities in BookingForm.
issues.root_cause: TypeScript strict mode violations and incomplete type definitions
evidence: 69 total TypeScript errors across components, forms, and pages
Key files: FreshAccommodationDisplay.tsx, ManageHotelForm.tsx, BookingForm.tsx, MyBookings.tsx
issues.fix_suggestion: 1) Add missing type definitions to BookingType interface, 2) Remove unused imports and variables, 3) Replace implicit any types with proper types, 4) Fix type mismatches in form data

patch.diff: Multiple file changes required - see detailed fix suggestions

issues.title: Console.log statements in production code
issues.description: Found 35+ files with console.log/error/warn statements in backend and frontend source code. Console statements in production can expose sensitive information, impact performance, and clutter logs.
issues.root_cause: Debug statements not removed before production deployment
evidence: Backend: 35 files with console statements in src/
Frontend: 35 files with console statements in src/
Key files: index.ts, bootstrap.ts, various route handlers and components
issues.fix_suggestion: Replace console.log with proper logging library (winston, pino for backend; use logging service for frontend). Use environment variable to enable debug logs only in development.

patch.diff: Replace console.log with logger.log or remove in production builds

issues.title: Excessive use of 'any' types in TypeScript code
issues.description: Multiple files use 'any' types instead of proper TypeScript typing, defeating the purpose of TypeScript's type safety. Found in imageService.ts, bookingValidationService.ts, availabilityService.ts, and route handlers.
issues.root_cause: Incomplete type definitions and developer convenience over type safety
evidence: availabilityService.ts: multiple any types in query objects and parameters
issues.fix_suggestion: Define proper interfaces for all data structures. Replace any types with specific types or generics. Enable TypeScript strict mode to catch these at compile time.

patch.diff: Define interfaces: UploadedFile, BookingData, QueryConditions, etc.

issues.title: Missing unit and integration tests
issues.description: No unit or integration tests found in backend or frontend source directories. Only e2e tests exist in packages/e2e-tests. Critical business logic (booking validation, payment processing, auth) lacks test coverage.
issues.root_cause: Test infrastructure not set up for backend/frontend source code
issues.fix_suggestion: Set up Jest or Vitest for unit testing. Write tests for: authentication flow, booking validation, payment processing, availability checking, and API endpoints. Aim for 80%+ coverage.

patch.diff: Add test configuration and test files for critical paths

issues.title: Unused code and dead imports
issues.description: Multiple unused imports and variables throughout the codebase (40+ instances). This increases bundle size and reduces code maintainability.
evidence: Unused imports: Building2, Search, Star, DollarSign, AlertCircle, MessageSquare, etc.
Unused variables: isLoadingPlaces, hotelId, totalPrice, etc.
issues.fix_suggestion: Run ESLint with no-unused-vars rule enabled. Remove unused imports and variables. Use tree-shaking to eliminate dead code from production builds.

patch.diff: Remove unused imports and variables identified by TypeScript compiler

issues.title: Missing security headers configuration
issues.description: Backend uses Helmet middleware but may not have all recommended security headers configured. Frontend lacks Content Security Policy (CSP) configuration.
evidence: No explicit CSP configuration found in frontend
No HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options configuration visible
issues.fix_suggestion: Configure Helmet with strict security headers. Add CSP to frontend via meta tags or server configuration. Enable HSTS with appropriate max-age.

patch.diff: Add helmet configuration with contentSecurityPolicy, hsts, frameguard, etc.

manual_steps: 1. Remove hardcoded Stripe key from hotel-booking-frontend/.env and set up environment variable in deployment platform
2. Run 'npm audit fix' in both backend and frontend directories
3. For breaking changes, manually update vulnerable packages and test thoroughly
4. Fix TypeScript errors by adding missing type definitions to BookingType interface
5. Remove unused imports and variables (40+ instances)
6. Replace console.log statements with proper logging library
7. Set up Jest/Vitest and write unit tests for critical business logic
8. Replace 'any' types with proper TypeScript interfaces
actions.rollback_plan: 1. Git revert each fix individually if issues arise, 2. Keep backup of package.json files before dependency updates, 3. Test in staging environment before production deployment
